question: when was the last time anyone was on the moon
answer: The last time anyone was on the moon was during the Apollo 17 mission in December 1972. Astronauts Eugene Cernan and Harrison Schmitt spent about three days on the lunar surface, while Ron Evans orbited overhead in the Command Module. Cernan was the last person to leave the moon, and no humans have returned since then. question: what is the maximum data rate for the 802.11a standard select one
answer: The maximum data rate for the 802.11a standard is 54 Mbps. This standard operates in the 5 GHz frequency band and uses orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) encoding. It is one of the first wireless local area network (WLAN) standards to be released and provides higher data rates and improved performance compared to the 802.11b standard.
